channel.title: Leadership on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Leadership on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: When To Step Out Of The Way
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about when to step out of the way — or my alternate title, which was way too long to fit on the title card — Things I didn’t do as much as didn’t stop from happening, which isn’t something that I said.

It’s what somebody said to me It’s, I’ll give you. I’ll tell you that story that this, video, is probably about the single most important thing that you can learn how to do if you want to be a leader, if you want to be the lead programmer or lead artist or lead designer of a group, if you want to be a producer or, if it happens, you’re a game director. And that most important thing that you need to learn how to do, above everything else, is when to just get out of the way and let the people who are on your team do what they do best.

item.title: Definitions Are Hard (aka Why A Game Director Is Necessary)
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how definitions are hard and I’m going to subtitle this — why game director is necessary.

What I’m going to talk about today is why it is so incredibly hard to define something, a term, a concept, an object. People frequently argue because — not because they believe different things, but because they’re using different definitions of words, of Concepts, and they’re kind of talking past each other, and this happens a lot in the game industry and some of you are probably going — it’s not hard to define a term. That’s how you start, you define your terms and then you move on from there. No, it is really hard to define something and people will disagree on the most basic, fundamental of definitions. So I want to tell you a story first before I dive into the game development parts of here. Really, Tim, tell a story, so the very first day of an AI class I had in college, the teacher posed a question to the class, and this was a 90-minute class and this is all we talked about.

item.title: Consistency In Leadership
item.description:
Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about consistency in leadership, and maybe a better way to put that would be: if you’re going to be a good leader, you have to be consistent.

It’s what people expect. It’s prob of the first thing you should think about yourself. If you’re thinking about stepping into a lead role or being a director or being a producer, are you con consistent? The problem with not being consistent is, if you decide differently for different people, you will either be inconsistent, which will drive people crazy, or you’ll be perceived as inconsistent, which will still drive people crazy, even if you’re not being inconsistent.
